title: Remove List Items
content:
### Removing Items

---

### `.remove()` -- Remove by Value

```python
fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
fruits.remove("banana")
print(fruits)  # ["apple", "cherry"]
```

Removes only the **first occurrence**.

> **Error:** Raises `ValueError` if the item does not exist.

---

### `.pop()` -- Remove by Index

```python
fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
removed = fruits.pop(1)
print(removed)  # "banana"
print(fruits)   # ["apple", "cherry"]

# Without index, removes last item
last = fruits.pop()
print(last)  # "cherry"
```

`.pop()` **returns** the removed item.

---

### `del` Keyword -- Remove by Index or Slice

```python
fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
del fruits[0]
print(fruits)  # ["banana", "cherry"]

# Delete entire list
del fruits
# print(fruits)  # NameError!
```

---

### `.clear()` -- Empty the List

```python
fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry"]
fruits.clear()
print(fruits)  # []
```

---

> **Difference:** `remove()` deletes by value, `pop()` deletes by index (and returns it), `del` deletes by index or variable, `clear()` empties the list.
codeExample:
fruits = ["apple", "banana", "cherry", "banana"]

fruits.remove("banana")  # Removes first banana
print("After remove:", fruits)

removed = fruits.pop(0)
print("Popped:", removed)
print("After pop:", fruits)

last = fruits.pop()
print("Last popped:", last)

fruits.clear()
print("After clear:", fruits)

quiz.question: Which method removes an item by its value?
